# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment that mounts host directories for our logging agent. The pod needs access to /var/log and /var/lib/docker to collect logs from all containers. This is for our centralized logging system that runs as a DaemonSet.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: log-agent-deployment
spec:
  replicas: 3 # number of pods to maintain alive and running logs collection process, you can modify it based on your requirements. If more Pods are needed just increase the value here accordingly (default is three). Minimum recommended for production use case could be one or two if feasible; in a high-scale deployment environment they might serve as active monitors of services that require frequent log accessibility 
  selector: # Selector to keep track on which pod(s) are using this Deployment. The Pod Name is included here, and it has an effect when you include the template section below where spec (the specification about how your container should behave).   It'll get updated by a Kubernetes rolling update process
    matchLabels:  # Match Selector to track labels on pod templates used within this Deployment. The name 'log-agent'- included here and above also has an effect when you include the template section below where spec (the specification about how your container should behave). It'll get updated by a Kubernetes rolling update process
      app: log_agent  # Label that must match to identify Pods using this Deployment. If 'log-agent'- included here and above also has an effect when you include the template section below where spec (the specification about how your container should behave). It'll get updated by a Kubernetes rolling update process
      log_type: agent  # Label that must match to identify Pods using this Deployment. If 'log-agent'- included here and above also has an effect when you include the template section below where spec (the specification about how your container should behave). It'll get updated by a Kubernetes rolling update process
template:   
  metadata:     # Meta information of pods to be created, including name(log-agent), namespace in which it will exist and labels that identify the Template. This data does not influence behavior but is needed for managing Pod creation within this Deployment template context  
      labels:       # Add a Label called 'app'=Log_Agent & another label of type as mentioned above  logType to make identification easy on rollouts, retries and updates etc... Recommended are app : Log-agent. Also included here for ease in use with other kubernetes resources  
        - name: podlogs    # Name assigned at creation time (default is 'pod') & can be named as per requirement  log_type= agent      app：Log Agent         
        
       annotations :# Adding a note that this template creates Pod, in case there are any customizations or additional configuration required for the pods. This data does not influence behavior but is needed to understand how your container should behave  
        - "created-by":  kubectl    # Name of tool used at creation time (default 'kubectl') & can be changed as per requirement, e ,g., minikube or kind for use in the specific cluster. Also included here to allow easy discovery and tracking purpose  
         - "created-by" : k8s  # Tool that created this configuration ('minikube' currently default) For ease of management & can be adjusted as per your needs e,g., minio or etcd for use in the specific environment. Also included here to allow easy discovery and tracking purpose  
      name: log-agent    # Name assigned at creation time (default is 'log_Agent')  - a unique id that will distinguish between Pods within this Deployment from outside its definition, but can be adjusted according as per need e ,g., with prefix or suffix in the case of multiple pod replicas
